name: Branch and ATM Locator
description: Caller asks for nearest branch or ATM location based on address, suburb, or postal code. AI responds with location details, operating hours, and available services.
permitted_actions: Search location database; return branch/ATM details; offer to send SMS with directions
prohibited_actions: Disclose any account information; access customer records
human_handoff_triggers: Request for services not available at suggested branch; accessibility requirements
required_components: RAG Service; Conversation Runtime; basic Policy Engine
success_metrics: Containment rate; caller satisfaction; location accuracy
regulatory_considerations: Minimal. Accessibility requirements for visually impaired callers.
evaluation_scenarios: Ambiguous location queries; locations near branch coverage gaps; after-hours queries; multilingual location names

name: Product FAQ from Approved Knowledge
description: Caller asks about banking products (savings accounts, credit cards, loans, mortgages). AI responds from approved knowledge base only. No personalized recommendations. No suitability assessment.
permitted_actions: Search approved product knowledge base; provide factual product information; offer to connect to product specialist
prohibited_actions: Recommend specific products; provide personalized rates or pricing; assess suitability; compare products in ways that constitute advice; disclose any account information
human_handoff_triggers: Questions crossing into advice territory; questions about product suitability; questions about specific rates or personalized pricing; caller expressing financial difficulty
required_components: RAG Service (approved-content-only with citation tracking); Conversation Runtime; Policy Engine (advice boundary detection)
success_metrics: Containment rate; advice boundary violation rate; caller satisfaction
regulatory_considerations: Financial advice boundary is jurisdictionally sensitive. MAS, MiFID II (EU), FCA (UK), FINRA (US) all restrict personalized product recommendations. Policy Engine must detect and block advice-boundary crossings. RAG must return only bank-approved content.
evaluation_scenarios: Questions subtly crossing from information to advice; outdated product information; competitor product questions; T&C nuances

name: Opening Hours and Appointment Booking
description: Caller asks about branch/department hours. If booking is available, AI can schedule an appointment. Hours portion is A0, appointment booking is A4.
permitted_actions: Provide hours; check appointment availability; book appointment slots
prohibited_actions: Access account information; change account settings
required_authentication_base: AUTH_0 for hours; AUTH_2 for booking if linked to account
fraud_controls: None for hours; caller verification for booking
human_handoff_triggers: Complex booking requirements; accessibility needs; branch not found
backend_systems: Branch hours database; appointment booking system
required_components: RAG Service (hours/calendar); Tool Gateway (booking system write for A4); Conversation Runtime
success_metrics: Containment rate; booking completion rate; no-show rate
evaluation_scenarios: Holiday schedules; temporary closures; booking conflicts; time zone handling

name: Intelligent Intent Classification and Routing
description: Replaces legacy IVR tree with natural language intent classification. Caller states reason for calling. AI classifies intent, applies routing rules, and transfers to appropriate queue, workflow, or specialist.
permitted_actions: Classify intent; determine routing; provide estimated wait times; offer callback; route call
prohibited_actions: Disclose account data; attempt to resolve issues beyond routing; promise specific outcomes
required_authentication_base: AUTH_0 (none initially); may request basic verification for routing to authenticated queues
human_handoff_triggers: Unrecognized intent after two attempts; caller frustration; caller requesting specific person; emergency (threat, fraud in progress)
backend_systems: ACD/routing system; queue management; workforce management
required_components: Conversation Runtime (intent classification); Policy Engine (routing rules); Human Control Center (queue management)
success_metrics: Routing accuracy; first-contact resolution; misroute rate; AHT reduction
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; intent_classification_accuracy; emergency_detection; vulnerability_detection; noisy_audio; accent_suite
regulatory_considerations: Call recording consent at start. Vulnerability detection active from first utterance for appropriate routing.
evaluation_scenarios: Multi-intent calls; ambiguous intent; emotional callers; callers refusing to state intent; code-switching; noisy audio; callers reading from scripts (social engineering)

name: Balance and Recent Transactions
description: Authenticated caller requests account balance and/or recent transaction history. AI retrieves from core banking and reads back.
permitted_actions: Authenticate caller; retrieve balance; retrieve last N transactions; read transaction details
prohibited_actions: Disclose full account numbers; disclose other accounts not explicitly requested; provide financial advice based on balance; disclose to unauthorized callers
fraud_controls: Caller risk score; account risk score; step-up on high risk; human transfer on critical risk
human_handoff_triggers: Authentication failure; suspicious activity on account; caller disputes transaction; caller distress about balance
backend_systems: Core banking (balance API); transaction history API; authentication/identity service; fraud risk scoring
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (core banking read); Policy Engine (disclosure rules, PCI redaction); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
retention_policy: Bank policy call recordings; PCI-compliant for card data
success_metrics: Containment rate; unauthorized disclosure rate; authentication success rate; AHT
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; authentication_bypass_testing; disclosure_policy_testing; PCI_redaction_testing; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite
regulatory_considerations: Balance is account-specific PII. Must verify identity before disclosure. PCI DSS applies if card numbers appear. Audit trail must record what was disclosed, to whom, when. Some jurisdictions restrict recording of full card numbers.
evaluation_scenarios: Multiple accounts; joint accounts; overdrawn accounts; callers asking about unrecognized transactions; PCI-sensitive data in descriptions

name: Application Status Inquiry
description: Caller checks status of a pending application (credit card, loan, account opening). AI retrieves status from application processing system.
permitted_actions: Authenticate caller; retrieve application status; provide expected timeline; explain next steps
prohibited_actions: Disclose application decision rationale (for credit products); change application details; expedite processing; disclose to non-applicant
human_handoff_triggers: Application rejected (caller needs explanation); application stuck/delayed; caller wants to modify application
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (application system read); Policy Engine; Conversation Runtime
success_metrics: Containment rate; caller satisfaction; repeat call rate
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; authentication_bypass_testing; disclosure_policy_testing; PCI_redaction_testing; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite
regulatory_considerations: Credit decision explanations may trigger adverse action notice requirements (US ECOA/Reg B, UK FCA). AI must not explain credit decisions.
evaluation_scenarios: Multiple pending applications; joint applications; different stages; callers wanting reasons for delays

name: Complaint Intake
description: Caller wishes to lodge a complaint. AI captures complaint details, classifies the complaint type, creates a case record, and confirms reference number. AI does not resolve the complaint.
permitted_actions: Authenticate caller; capture complaint narrative; classify complaint category; create case in complaint system; provide reference number; set response timeline expectations
prohibited_actions: Resolve or adjudicate the complaint; offer compensation; admit liability; promise specific outcomes; dismiss the complaint
required_disclosures: Recording notice; complaint process disclosure; expected response timeline
human_handoff_triggers: Caller distress or anger; complaint involves potential regulatory breach; complaint about AI itself; caller requests supervisor; vulnerability indicators
backend_systems: Complaint management system; CRM; case routing system
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (complaint system write - draft); Policy Engine (complaint classification, vulnerability detection); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger (complete recording and transcript)
retention_policy: Complaint retention policy (regulatory minimum varies by jurisdiction)
success_metrics: Complaint capture completeness; classification accuracy; caller satisfaction; time to create case
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; case_creation_validation; vulnerability_detection; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests
regulatory_considerations: UK FCA requires complaints acknowledged, investigated, resolved within prescribed timeframes. MAS requires fair and prompt handling. US CFPB tracks patterns. AI role is intake only. Record must be tamper-evident and auditable. Vulnerability detection mandatory throughout.
evaluation_scenarios: Emotionally charged callers; multi-issue complaints; fee overlap complaints; third-party complaints; caller wrong but must be handled respectfully; systemic issues

name: Lost or Stolen Card - Block and Replace
description: Caller reports card lost or stolen. AI authenticates, blocks the card immediately (urgent action to prevent fraud), initiates replacement card, and creates case notes.
permitted_actions: Authenticate; block card; initiate replacement; confirm replacement delivery address; disclose replacement timeline; create case note
prohibited_actions: Disclose full card number; change customer address; initiate money movement; process pending transactions
required_disclosures: Recording notice; replacement fee disclosure if applicable
fraud_controls: Caller risk score; account risk score; step-up on high risk; human transfer on critical risk
human_handoff_triggers: Suspected account takeover; caller reports unauthorized transactions (escalate to fraud); tool error during card block; caller distress
backend_systems: Card processor (block API, replacement API); CRM; fraud case system; notification service
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (card processor write); Policy Engine (risk assessment, step-up rules); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
retention_policy: Bank policy call recordings; fraud retention
success_metrics: Card block time; containment rate; unauthorized action rate; AHT; fraud escape rate in simulation
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests; rollback_testing
regulatory_considerations: Card blocking is consumer-protective. Urgency justifies faster authentication. Replacement requires stricter identity verification (address disclosure, new card issuance). PCI DSS applies. Replacement fees must be disclosed before processing.
evaluation_scenarios: Social engineering (calling about mothers card); callers without card details; multiple cards on account; joint accounts; international callers; recent address changes; noisy audio during verification

name: Card Activation
description: Caller received a new or replacement card and wants to activate it. AI verifies identity with step-up authentication, activates the card, and confirms.
permitted_actions: Authenticate with step-up; activate card; confirm activation; set temporary spending limits if applicable
prohibited_actions: Change PIN; disclose full card number; modify account settings
fraud_controls: Step-up authentication mandatory; fraud risk scoring; suspicious activation detection
human_handoff_triggers: Authentication failure; card not found in system; card already activated; suspicious activity
backend_systems: Card processor (activation API); authentication service (OTP/push); CRM
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (card processor activation API); Policy Engine (step-up enforcement); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Activation success rate; containment rate; AHT; fraud detection rate
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests; rollback_testing
regulatory_considerations: Card activation requires strong authentication per PCI DSS and card scheme rules.
evaluation_scenarios: Caller without phone for OTP; activating card they did not request (potential fraud); replacement card activation while old still active

name: Statement Request
description: Caller requests a copy of a recent statement. AI authenticates, confirms statement period, verifies delivery preference, and submits request.
permitted_actions: Authenticate; confirm statement period; verify delivery address/email; submit statement request; provide expected delivery timeline
prohibited_actions: Read out full statement contents over phone; change delivery address; access statements older than retention period
required_authentication_step_up: AUTH_3 for delivery to non-default address
human_handoff_triggers: Statement period not available; caller wants statement for closed account; caller disputes statement contents
backend_systems: Statement generation system; fulfillment/delivery service; CRM
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (statement fulfillment API); Policy Engine (delivery controls); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests; rollback_testing
regulatory_considerations: Statement delivery to non-registered addresses requires additional verification. Postal delivery must comply with data protection (no account details on envelope).
evaluation_scenarios: Multiple account types; historical statements beyond standard range; delivery to shared email (privacy); callers wanting statements read aloud

name: Fraud Alert Confirmation
description: Bank fraud system flagged a transaction. Caller contacts bank to confirm or deny. If legitimate, AI clears alert (A4). If fraud, AI escalates to investigation (A5 - human review).
permitted_actions: Authenticate with step-up; present flagged transaction (without full card number); clear alert if confirmed legitimate; escalate to fraud if denied; block card if fraud confirmed and customer requests
prohibited_actions: Disclose full transaction details before authentication; clear fraud alerts without proper authentication; process refunds; conclude fraud investigation
fraud_controls: Caller risk score; account risk score; step-up on high risk; human transfer on critical risk; APP scam detection
human_handoff_triggers: Caller denies transaction (fraud confirmed); multiple flagged transactions; high-value transaction; caller distress; authentication failure; suspected social engineering
backend_systems: Fraud detection system; card processor; case management; fraud investigation queue
required_components: All components including Fraud-Aware Identity (critical); Tool Gateway (fraud system APIs); Policy Engine (fraud-specific rules); Human Control Center (fraud team escalation); Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Containment rate; fraud detection rate; false positive resolution rate; customer satisfaction; unauthorized action rate
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; APP_scam_simulation; social_engineering; synthetic_voice_testing; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests
regulatory_considerations: Fraud handling heavily regulated. MAS, FCA, CFPB have specific requirements. Unauthorized transaction liability varies by jurisdiction. AI must not make liability determinations. Safe callback protocol required for outbound fraud alerts.
evaluation_scenarios: Authorized push payment scams; synthetic voice attacks; callers confused about flagged transaction; callers not remembering transaction; simultaneous fraud on multiple cards

name: Transaction Dispute Intake
description: Caller disputes a specific transaction. AI captures dispute details, classifies dispute type (unauthorized, goods not received, service not as described), captures evidence, creates dispute case. Resolution is human-handled.
permitted_actions: Authenticate; capture dispute details; classify dispute type; capture evidence; create dispute case
prohibited_actions: Resolve dispute; issue refunds; make liability determinations; issue provisional credit without human approval
required_disclosures: Recording notice; dispute process disclosure; regulatory timelines
fraud_controls: Caller risk score; dispute pattern analysis
human_handoff_triggers: Multiple disputes; high-value dispute; unclear dispute type; caller distress
backend_systems: Card processor dispute API; case management; scheme portal (Visa/Mastercard)
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (dispute system write - draft); Policy Engine (dispute classification, scheme rules); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Dispute capture completeness; classification accuracy; time to create case
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; case_creation_validation; vulnerability_detection; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests
regulatory_considerations: Dispute classification is complex (multiple scheme rules). Provisional credit decisions have financial impact. Regulatory timelines are strict (Regulation E in US, PSR in UK).
evaluation_scenarios: Multi-dispute calls; high-value disputes; ambiguous dispute types; callers with multiple affected cards
deferred_reason: Dispute classification complexity across scheme rules; provisional credit decisions have financial impact; strict regulatory timelines (Reg E, PSR)

name: Fee Waiver Request
description: Caller requests waiver of a fee (late payment, overdraft, annual fee). AI checks fee waiver policy, auto-approves below threshold (A4), escalates above threshold to supervisor (A5).
permitted_actions: Authenticate; check fee waiver policy; auto-approve below threshold; escalate above threshold; confirm waiver
prohibited_actions: Override policy limits; waive fees without proper authorization; make promises about future fees
human_handoff_triggers: Amount above threshold; repeat waiver requests; caller escalation; complex fee structures
backend_systems: Fee management system; CRM (customer value/tenure); approval workflow
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (fee management); Policy Engine (threshold logic, explainability); Human Control Center (supervisor approval for A5); Audit Ledger
retention_policy: Standard call recording; waiver audit trail
success_metrics: Waiver approval rate; escalation rate; customer retention impact; revenue impact
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; APP_scam_simulation; social_engineering; synthetic_voice_testing; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests
regulatory_considerations: Fee waiver policies vary by bank. Threshold logic requires bank-specific configuration. Fairness monitoring needed to prevent discriminatory waiver patterns.
evaluation_scenarios: Threshold boundary cases; repeat waiver attempts; high-value versus low-value customers; callers with hardship
deferred_reason: Fee waiver policies vary by bank; threshold logic requires bank-specific configuration; fairness monitoring needed to prevent discriminatory patterns

name: Contact Detail Update
description: Caller wants to update contact details (address, email, phone). AI authenticates with strong step-up, confirms changes, applies with risk hold if applicable.
permitted_actions: Authenticate with strong step-up; confirm changes; apply update with risk hold; notify old contact method
prohibited_actions: Update without strong authentication; bypass risk hold; update for third parties
required_disclosures: Recording notice; change confirmation; old-method notification
fraud_controls: Fraud risk scoring (address change is high-risk); notification to old contact method; cooling-off period; human review for flagged accounts
human_handoff_triggers: Authentication failure; flagged account; multiple recent changes; suspicious circumstances
backend_systems: CRM (contact update API); authentication service; fraud risk engine; notification service
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer (critical); Tool Gateway (CRM update); Policy Engine (risk hold, cooling-off rules); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Update success rate; fraud detection rate; false positive rate
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests; rollback_testing
regulatory_considerations: Contact detail changes are the top account takeover vector. Requires robust fraud controls and step-up authentication. Social engineering risk is very high.
evaluation_scenarios: Social engineering attempts; recently compromised accounts; high-risk address changes; multiple simultaneous changes; callers under duress
deferred_reason: Contact detail changes are the number one account takeover vector. Requires robust fraud controls and step-up authentication before deployment.

name: Collections Reminder (Inbound)
description: Customer in arrears calls about their account. AI provides current balance, arrears amount, and payment options. Can set up payment arrangements within pre-approved templates.
permitted_actions: Authenticate; provide balance and arrears information; present payment options; set up payment arrangements within templates; refer to hardship support
prohibited_actions: Threaten or pressure caller; misrepresent consequences; set up arrangements outside approved templates; bypass vulnerability checks
required_disclosures: Recording notice; rights disclosure; hardship support availability
human_handoff_triggers: Vulnerability indicators; hardship disclosure; caller distress; arrangement outside templates; caller disputes the debt
backend_systems: Collections system; payment arrangement system; hardship referral; vulnerability flagging
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (collections system); Policy Engine (jurisdictional script controls, vulnerability detection); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Arrangement completion rate; vulnerability detection rate; compliance rate; caller satisfaction
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; vulnerability_detection; jurisdictional_script_testing; fraud_simulation; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests
regulatory_considerations: Collections heavily regulated with jurisdiction-specific requirements. Vulnerability detection is critical (callers in financial difficulty are by definition vulnerable). FCA, CFPB, MAS, ASIC all have specific collections conduct requirements.
evaluation_scenarios: Vulnerable callers; callers in severe hardship; callers disputing debt; multi-product arrears; callers under third-party pressure
deferred_reason: Heavily regulated with jurisdiction-specific requirements. Vulnerability detection critical. FCA, CFPB, MAS, ASIC all have specific collections conduct rules.

name: Travel Notification
description: Caller notifies bank of upcoming travel to prevent card blocks in foreign countries.
permitted_actions: Authenticate; capture destination and dates; apply travel notification to card/fraud rules
prohibited_actions: Modify card limits without explicit request; change account settings beyond travel flag
human_handoff_triggers: Complex itinerary; multiple cards; caller requests not supported by travel system
backend_systems: Card processor (travel notification API); fraud rules engine
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (card processor); Policy Engine; Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Notification success rate; containment rate; AHT
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests; rollback_testing
regulatory_considerations: Minimal regulatory exposure. Standard authentication and fraud policy integration required.
evaluation_scenarios: Multi-destination travel; extended periods; multiple cards; overlapping dates; last-minute travel
deferred_reason: Lower priority; many banks handle via mobile app. Simple workflow but requires card processor integration.

name: Card Replacement (Non-Lost)
description: Caller requests replacement card (damaged, expired, name change). Not an emergency unlike UC-008 (lost card).
permitted_actions: Authenticate with step-up; verify address; disclose replacement fee; process replacement; confirm delivery timeline
prohibited_actions: Change address (separate workflow); bypass fee disclosure; expedite without authorization
required_disclosures: Recording notice; replacement fee; delivery timeline
human_handoff_triggers: Address change needed; fee dispute; card type change; complex replacement
backend_systems: Card processor (replacement API); CRM; fee management
required_components: Fraud-Aware Identity Layer; Tool Gateway (card processor); Policy Engine (fee disclosure, address verification); Conversation Runtime; Audit Ledger
success_metrics: Replacement completion rate; containment rate; AHT
pre_release_tests: golden_calls; adversarial_prompt_injection; fraud_simulation; social_engineering; noisy_audio; accent_suite; policy_violation_tests; rollback_testing
regulatory_considerations: Standard authentication and PCI DSS. Fee disclosure mandatory before processing.
evaluation_scenarios: Address mismatch; fee edge cases; multiple card types; name change verifications; callers wanting expedited delivery
deferred_reason: Overlaps with UC-008. Implement after UC-008 proves stable.
